---
title: "Arkansas Weekly Weather & Soil Water Reports"
subtitle: "Automated Intelligence for Crop Development"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

## Welcome

This site provides **automated, weekly reports** on Arkansas weather conditions and soil water status designed to support agricultural decision-making for researchers and farmers.

Every Monday morning, we update comprehensive analyses comparing current conditions to 20+ years of historical data, with 7-10 day forecasts and actionable crop development metrics.

---

## Key Features

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 📊 Benchmarked Data
Compare current conditions to historical normals (1995–2020). Understand how this week ranks among historical records.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 💧 Soil Water Monitoring
Track relative soil water status across research stations. Identify crop stress risk zones in real-time.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 🌦️ Weather Forecasts
7-10 day forecasts for major research stations. Frost risk, precipitation probability, and stress projections.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### 📍 Multi-Station Coverage
Monitor 10+ Arkansas research stations. Statewide grid analysis at 5 km resolution.
:::
:::

---

## Latest Report

::: {.callout-tip}
## Current Week Report

**Week of {{< meta report_week >}}** — Last updated {{< meta report_date >}}

[**📄 View Full Report**](reports/current-report.qmd) | [📥 Download Data](data-downloads.qmd)

:::

### Statewide Summary

- **Temperature:** Check the current report for this week's high/low temperatures and anomalies
- **Precipitation:** See how this week's rainfall compares to normal conditions
- **Soil Water:** Monitor relative soil water status across the state
- **Forecast:** 7-day outlook with crop development impacts

---

## How to Use This Site

1. **Check the Current Report** — Start with [this week's report](reports/current-report.qmd) for the full analysis
2. **Explore Station Details** — Each major research station has a detailed profile with historical context
3. **Download Data** — Access CSV datasets for further analysis ([Data Downloads](data-downloads.qmd))
4. **Review Archives** — Explore [past reports](reports/archive.qmd) to see seasonal trends
5. **Lab Updates** — Follow [lab news and research findings](lab-updates.qmd) related to weather impacts

---

## Data Sources

| Data Type | Source | Update Frequency |
|-----------|--------|------------------|
| **Current Weather** | IEM / NOAA | Daily |
| **Weather Forecasts** | Open-Meteo | Daily |
| **Historical Climate** | NOAA / NASS | Annual |
| **Soil Properties** | USDA SSURGO | Annual |

All data is publicly available and used with permission.

---

## Understanding the Reports

### Temperature & Precipitation Anomalies
We compare each week to the historical average (1995–2020 baseline).
- **Percentile ranking:** Where does this week rank among 20+ years of data?
- **Above/below normal:** How unusual is this week's conditions?
- **Record extremes:** Is this a historically hot/wet/dry week?

### Soil Water Status
Our bucket model tracks:
- **Current soil moisture** — Water available to plants (mm)
- **Relative soil water** — As a percentage of available water capacity (AWC)
- **Stress indicator** — Days when plants may experience water stress

### Growing Degree Days (GDD)
Accumulated heat units (base 50°F) since April 1. Useful for:
- **Crop development timing** — When to expect flowering, physiological maturity
- **Pest pressure** — Insect development linked to GDD
- **Year comparison** — How this season compares to historical normals

---

## Contact & Feedback

**Project Lead:** [Dr. Name](mailto:eelli@uark.edu)
**Lab:** University of Arkansas Division of Agriculture
**GitHub:** [ar-weekly-weather-soil-reports](https://github.com/your-lab/ar-weekly-weather-soil-reports)

We welcome feedback on report content, data sources, or suggestions for new metrics. Email us or [open an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/your-lab/ar-weekly-weather-soil-reports/issues).

---

## Technical Details

- **Language:** R (data processing), Quarto (reporting)
- **APIs:** Open-Meteo, Iowa Environmental Mesonet (IEM)
- **Update Schedule:** Every Monday 7:00 AM (Central Time)
- **Report Runtime:** ~15 minutes
- **Repository:** [GitHub](https://github.com/your-lab/ar-weekly-weather-soil-reports)

See [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/your-lab/ar-weekly-weather-soil-reports/blob/main/CLAUDE.md) for detailed developer documentation.

---

**Last updated:** {{< meta report_date >}}
